description: Behavioral evals for the anchored lifecycle skills, run against the anchored-test sandbox via /loop (one eval per iteration). Evals 1-4 are a CHAINED lifecycle on Feature A — each stage consumes the prior stage's task-file, so they must run in order. Eval 5 exercises the /impl autopilot end-to-end on a separate Feature B. Eval 6 (Feature C, independent + config-mutating) verifies the use:/type/instructions step-dispatch: it seeds a wrap-status task plus a probe `use:` wrap step, runs /impl-wrap, and checks the dispatched agent's observable marker — restoring anchored.yml afterward. The gradeable artifact for every eval is the resulting .claude/tasks/<slug>.yml (status, phase/AC statuses, evidence, resolved questions, context.build/​context.wrap audit trail) plus the real source diff in the sandbox.
mcp: Tools resolve as mcp__anchored__task__* (project .mcp.json → local dev build) or mcp__plugin_anchored_task__task__* (loaded plugin); either works.
reset: Before the FIRST eval of a fresh suite run: delete any prior suite task-files (slugs containing 'filter' or 'clear-completed') from .claude/tasks/ in the sandbox. Do NOT touch tasks-list-persistence.yml (Jack's separate drafted task) or _archive/. Snapshot each stage's task-file into evals/results/<run>/fixtures/ after it passes so an isolated (non-chained) suite can reuse them later.
autonomy: Run every skill non-interactively so the loop never blocks: /impl-refine uses walk-style AI-all (AI resolves every question with reasoning); /impl-build runs in its default autonomous mode (proceeds+documents, stops only on a build.stop plan-deviation). If a skill WOULD escalate to the user (a genuine stop / blocking question), do NOT answer it — record it as the eval's outcome ('halted: <reason>') and move on. A halt is a finding, not a loop failure.

evals.precondition: No sandbox task whose slug contains 'filter' exists.
evals.prompt: /impl-plan add a filter bar to the todo app with three modes — All / Active / Completed — that controls which todos are visible, and remember the selected mode across page reloads
evals.expected_output: A new .claude/tasks/<filter-slug>.yml at status 'drafted' with 2+ phases, every phase carrying testable acceptance criteria, and at least one open priority-tagged question surfaced (not silently decided).
assertions: A new task-file matching the filter feature was created in the sandbox .claude/tasks/
Task status is exactly 'drafted'
The task has at least 2 phases
Every phase has at least one acceptance criterion with non-empty text
Every acceptance criterion is status 'pending' with no evidence (drafted carries no proof)
At least one question exists with status 'open'
No source files in the sandbox were modified (plan does not write code)

evals.precondition: Eval 1 passed — the filter task exists at status 'drafted' with open questions.
evals.prompt: /impl-refine <filter-slug>  (walk-style: AI-all — resolve every open question via AI with reasoning, no user prompts)
evals.expected_output: Status flips drafted → refined; every open question becomes 'resolved' (source 'ai' entries carry reasoning); plan-check and rules-check rollups land in context.build; no acceptance-criterion text is mangled and no phase is silently dropped.
assertions: Task status is exactly 'refined'
Zero questions remain at status 'open'
Every resolved question has a non-empty answer; every source='ai' resolution also has non-empty reasoning
context.build contains a 'plan-check' section and a 'rules-check' section
Phase count is unchanged from the drafted state (or any change is traceable in the plan log, not silent)
All acceptance-criterion texts from the drafted state are still present

evals.precondition: Eval 2 passed — the filter task is at status 'refined'.
evals.expected_output: Every phase reaches a terminal state (done/blocked/deferred); done-phases have all ACs proven with non-empty evidence; task-validate and code-validate rollups land in context.build; the sandbox source (app.js / index.html / style.css) is actually modified to implement the filter; task status flips build → wrap.
assertions: Task status is exactly 'wrap'
Every phase is in a terminal state (done, blocked, or deferred)
At least one phase reached 'done' (the build produced real progress, not all-blocked)
For every phase at status 'done', every acceptance criterion is status 'done' with non-empty evidence
context.build contains both a 'task-validate' and a 'code-validate' section
Sandbox source files were actually modified (git diff shows changes to app.js or index.html or style.css)
The implemented filter is functionally present in the code (All/Active/Completed visibility control + persisted selection)

evals.precondition: Eval 3 passed — the filter task is at status 'wrap'.
evals.expected_output: A final review pass runs; a TL;DR summary is written to context.wrap; all phases remain terminal; task status flips wrap → done.
assertions: Task status is exactly 'done'
All phases are in a terminal state
context.wrap contains a non-empty summary / TL;DR
Any blocked phases (if present) are surfaced in the wrap summary for human attention

evals.precondition: No sandbox task whose slug contains 'clear-completed' exists. Independent of Feature A.
evals.prompt: /impl add a 'Clear completed' button that deletes all completed todos in one click; it should be disabled when there are no completed todos
evals.expected_output: The autopilot sequences plan → refine → build → wrap on its own. It either drives the task to status 'done' (all phases terminal, source implements the button), or halts cleanly at a documented blocking question / stop — and the halt is recorded rather than silently swallowed.
assertions: A new task-file matching the clear-completed feature was created
The task advanced beyond 'drafted' (the autopilot chained at least into refine/build, not stopping after plan)
If the run completed: status is 'done', all phases terminal, and the 'Clear completed' button is present in the sandbox source
If the run halted: the halt reason is captured as the eval outcome (which skill/phase, why) — a halt is a finding, not a crash
context.build contains plan-check + rules-check + task-validate + code-validate sections (evidence the full chain executed)

evals.precondition: Independent of A/B. Requires a one-time SETUP before running and a TEARDOWN after grading (see fields below) — this is the only eval that mutates anchored.yml, so it MUST restore the default config afterward or it corrupts evals 1-5.
setup: Seed the task: copy evals/results/fixtures/wrap-todo-filter-bar.yml into the sandbox .claude/tasks/ as usestep-dispatch-demo.yml, with its internal `slug:` rewritten to `usestep-dispatch-demo` and `title:` to a demo label. Status stays `wrap`; phases stay terminal.

Back up the sandbox anchored.yml, then set its wrap.steps to a single probe step: `- { name: dispatch-probe-agent, use: general-purpose, type: agent, instructions: "Create a file named USESTEP_AGENT.txt in the project root containing exactly this text and nothing else: agent-step-ran" }`.
evals.expected_output: The wrap pipeline dispatches the custom `use: general-purpose, type: agent` step as an ISOLATED subagent (Agent tool), threading `instructions` verbatim → USESTEP_AGENT.txt appears at the sandbox project root containing exactly `agent-step-ran`. The framework-default summarize still writes a non-empty TL;DR to context.wrap, and task status flips wrap → done. The step is NOT misinterpreted as a shell command and NOT silently skipped.
assertions: The use:-step was dispatched as an agent (not run as a shell command, not skipped): USESTEP_AGENT.txt exists at the sandbox project root
instructions were threaded into the worker verbatim: USESTEP_AGENT.txt content is exactly 'agent-step-ran' (trailing whitespace/newline ignored)
context.wrap contains a non-empty summary / TL;DR (the framework-default summarize still ran after the custom step)
Task status is exactly 'done'
All phases remain in a terminal state (the custom step did not regress phase/AC state)
teardown: Delete USESTEP_AGENT.txt and .claude/tasks/usestep-dispatch-demo.yml from the sandbox; restore the backed-up anchored.yml so the default-config evals (1-5) are unaffected.
